bullets: LSP and Hyosung hold offers; merchants undercut factory quotes.
NSRP 114G distributors cut to 35,500-36,200; one deal at 35,500.
IRPC and Sibur return with lower PP; Jinneng CIF may fall further.
STL HDPE Film FS7000 cut to 1,300-1,310; Sabic bids below offers.
Equate/Qamar HDPE Film to 41,000, some at 40,500 to clear.
Shintech SE950/SE1000 cut to 24,500-25,000 (some to ~34,500 quoted high).
AGC/TPC Vina hold June offers, focus on closing orders.

editorial.changedNote: Several PVC and PP/PE items eased on competition

summary: Foreign suppliers including STL and Sabic cut PE offers, yet buyers negotiated even lower finals.
Domestic sellers and factories marked down PE/PP; merchants undercut factory prices.
A large influx of Chinese resin en route fueled expectations of more declines into June.
paras: STL HDPE Film FS7000 to Vietnam fell from 1,340-1,350 to 1,300-1,310 CIF, yet buyers bid lower and were not rushing.

Sabic earlier offered June PE ex-Singapore: LDPE Film 1,650, coating 1,800, LLDPE Film 1,300-1,310, mLLDPE C6 1,700, HDPE Film 1,400, blow 1,450. Final prices ran below offers and bids: LLDPE bids from 1,250, LDPE from 1,550, HDPE from 1,350; one southern buyer signed Sabic HDPE Film at 1,330.
A northern source cited Qamar HDPE F1 finalized at 1,290 and JG Summit HDPE Film at 1,275 (ex-China), pending verification.
Sibur offered LLDPE Film 35,300 (down 900), mLLDPE 38,200, HDPE Film 37,800-38,000 (down 500-700), HDPE blow 37,000, HDPE injection 36,500-37,000.
LSP held LLDPE Film 38,300 South / 39,000 North and HDPE Film 41,000-41,200 South / 41,900 North; merchants offered LSP lower. At least two distributors cut Chinese oil-based 7042/7042N to 34,700.
Equate/Qamar HDPE Film to 41,000, some at 40,500 to clear; most HDPE yarn below 43,000. Lotte Titan 260GG to 50,000, one seller at 49,000.

summary: LSP and Hyosung held offers while merchants undercut factory quotes.
Northern distributors kept cutting NSRP 114G; one large deal at a market-low 35,500.
Thai, Russian and Chinese suppliers returned with lower PP; Chinese CIF may fall further.
paras: On May 27, LSP held PP homo 38,300 South / 39,000 North and copo 39,000-39,200 South / 39,900 North; merchants offered LSP lower. Hyosung held PP yarn ~37,400 with VAT and copo 39,600 ex-VAT. BSR T3034 at 36,800 HCMC (down 700).
In the North, at least two NSRP distributors kept cutting 114G: one offered 36,000-36,200, privately 35,800; another buyer signed 200 tons at 35,500 - the market low.
Oversupply returned; some distributors sold below market under cash-flow, debt or container-storage pressure.
Sibur PP homo at 36,000-36,300 (down 1,000 vs early last week); non-woven PP at 36,600.
IRPC re-offered after 2 months: yarn/injection 1,390 CFR HCMC, 1126NK 1,480, 1100RC 1,430, B1101 1,510.
Jinneng PP yarn HP550J/HP500N at 1,290 CIF (BOPP 1,300), but FOB China at 1,200 (BOPP 1,210), implying CIF could fall further (CIF usually adds 30-40).

summary: Domestic sellers slashed American PVC to clear stock, making buyers cautious.
Northern distributors kept cutting Chinese coal- and oil-based PVC to stay competitive.
AGC/TPC Vina held June offers, focusing on closing orders.
paras: On May 27, sellers in North and South cut Shintech SE950/SE1000 to 24,500-25,000, a deep drop from early week.
Fast price drops made buyers wary; some SE950 quoted as low as 34,500 still moved little.
In the North, Chinese SG-5 finalized to 22,000-22,100 to compete; Chinese oil-based offers to 24,300-24,500. Trading stayed slow.
AGC Vina and TPC Vina held June SP660/SG660/PM66R at 22,800-23,100 ex-VAT (~24,624-24,948 with VAT), focusing on closing orders.
